title: Google strikes back! Gemini Omni, AI glasses, new models
summary: Google released Gemini Omni, a new model supporting multimodal interactions. It represents Google's latest advancement in large language models and was announced at Google I/O.
writeup: A structured walkthrough of Google I/O's major AI releases, covering the multimodal video generation model Gemini Omni, the agentic-focused Gemini 3.5 Flash, the rebuilt Antigravity 2.0 coding platform, and the new Gemini Spark personal agent. The through-line is Google's shift from chatbot interfaces toward persistent, multi-agent systems that can work autonomously across hours-long tasks. Early benchmarks place Gemini 3.5 Flash behind GPT-5.5 and Opus 4.7 on independent leaderboards, but a Pro version is reported for the following month.
key_points: Gemini Omni is a multimodal video generation model that accepts any combination of text, image, video, and audio as input and can edit existing footage, replacing backgrounds, objects, and camera angles while maintaining visual consistency across generations; the presenter rates it below Kling and CogVideoX 2.0 for anatomy and high-action scenes in initial testing.

Gemini 3.5 Flash is positioned as Google's strongest agentic and coding model, claiming four times the output-token throughput of other frontier models, and outperforming Gemini 3.1 Pro on benchmarks including MCP Atlas, Charade reasoning, and MMMU Pro; a multi-agent demo recreated the AlphaZero research paper and produced a playable web app from two prompts in a few hours.

Antigravity 2.0 replaces the IDE paradigm with a chat-style multi-agent orchestration interface modeled after OpenAI Codex and Claude Code, and a demo produced a fully functional operating system that could run Doom in roughly 12 hours using Gemini 3.5 Flash.

On the independent Artificial Analysis leaderboard, Gemini 3.5 Flash ranks below GPT-5.5 and Opus 4.7, so the headline benchmark wins come from internal comparisons; the Pro tier, expected the following month, is the more direct frontier competitor.

Gemini Spark is a new cloud-based personal agent running on Gemini 3.5 that persists after the device is locked, integrating with Gmail, Docs, Slides, and Calendar, and is distinct from the daily-brief feature rolling out to Google Plus subscribers in the Gemini app.
